project: Royal Caribbean DAM — Enriched-First UX & Upload Review Flow
overview: Two UX improvements: (1) flip the asset detail modal to show enriched metadata first with an optional toggle to reveal original values; (2) replace the simple upload progress flow with a two-step Review carousel where users can inspect and edit AI-proposed metadata per image before committing to the database.
goals: Show enriched metadata as the primary view in AssetDetailModal with a toggle for original
Add a Review step to UploadModal: after enrichment, show a scrollable list of image cards with editable title, description, tags, location fields
Allow editing individual images and submitting one at a time, or editing all and submitting all at once
Hold all edits in local React state until user explicitly submits — nothing saved to DB until then
nonGoals: No new API endpoints — use existing POST /api/assets/:id/enrich and a new PATCH /api/assets/:id for user edits
No bulk re-enrichment from the UI
No editing of original_* fields — only enriched_* fields are updated on submit
No multi-step wizard outside of the UploadModal
successMetrics: AssetDetailModal defaults to showing enriched values; 'Show Original' toggle reveals side-by-side
After upload + enrichment, UploadModal advances to a Review step showing all uploaded images as editable cards
Submitting edits for a single image calls PATCH /api/assets/:id and shows a success state on that card
Submit All saves all edited images in sequence and shows a final summary

routes.name: Update Asset Enriched Fields
routes.purpose: Accept user-edited enriched fields (enriched_title, enriched_description, enriched_tags, enriched_location) and write them to the assets table. Sets enrichment_source='user-edited'.

uiNotes: AssetDetailModal: default view shows enriched values in a single-column layout. Add a 'Show Original' toggle button (top-right of metadata panel). When toggled on, render two-column layout (Original | Enriched) as before.
Enriched values shown in navy text (#001B6B). Empty enriched cells show 'Not enriched' in grey italic.
UploadModal steps: Step 1 = drop zone (existing), Step 2 = enrichment progress (existing per-file status), Step 3 = Review (new)
Auto-advance to Review step once ALL files have status 'done' or 'error'
Review step: scrollable vertical list of ReviewCard components, one per successfully enriched file
ReviewCard layout: left = thumbnail (150px square), right = editable fields (title, description, tags, location) + a 'Submit' button per card
Fields: title and description are single-line and multi-line text inputs respectively. Tags is a comma-separated text input. Location is a text input.
Each ReviewCard starts with AI-proposed values pre-filled. User can edit any field.
Per-card submit button label: 'Save' — on click calls PATCH /api/assets/:id, then shows green checkmark and disables the card
At top of Review step: 'Submit All' button — saves all unsubmitted cards in sequence, shows a progress count 'Saving X of Y...'
After all cards saved: show 'Done! X assets added to library.' with a 'View Assets' button that closes modal and refreshes grid
Error-state cards (enrichment failed) shown greyed out with message 'Enrichment failed — asset saved without AI metadata'. No submit button.
Review step header: 'Review & Edit Enriched Metadata' with a subtitle showing count: 'X images ready for review'

dataModel.description: PATCH endpoint updates enriched fields only
fields: enrichment_source TEXT — set to 'user-edited' on PATCH

rules: PATCH /api/assets/:id accepts body: { enriched_title, enriched_description, enriched_tags, enriched_location } — all optional, only provided fields are updated
enriched_tags in PATCH body may be a string (comma-separated) or array — normalize to JSON array string before saving
PATCH sets enrichment_source = 'user-edited' and updated_at = now()
PATCH updates FTS5 row after DB update (same trigger as UPDATE)
AssetDetailModal: 'Show Original' toggle state is local — resets to hidden each time modal opens
UploadModal: Review step only shows assets where upload succeeded (status was 'done' or 'enriching-done') — skip files that failed upload
ReviewCard edits are held in local state (useState per card) — not sent to API until Save or Submit All is clicked
Submit All iterates cards in order, calling PATCH sequentially — failure on one card logs it but continues to next

stories.title: PATCH /api/assets/:id — update enriched fields endpoint
stories.description: As a developer, I want a PATCH endpoint to update enriched metadata fields so user edits from the Review step can be persisted.
acceptanceCriteria: PATCH /api/assets/:id added to server/src/routes/assets.js
Accepts JSON body with any subset of: enriched_title (string), enriched_description (string), enriched_tags (string or array), enriched_location (string)
Normalizes enriched_tags: if array, JSON.stringify it; if comma-separated string, split/trim/JSON.stringify; if already JSON string, keep as-is
Updates only the fields provided in the request body — omitted fields are not changed
Sets enrichment_source = 'user-edited' and updated_at = current ISO timestamp
Returns the full updated asset record with 200 status
client/src/api/assets.js exports updateAsset(id, fields) that calls PATCH /api/assets/:id
Supertest test: PATCH with { enriched_title: 'New Title' } updates only enriched_title, leaves other fields unchanged
Supertest test: PATCH with enriched_tags as array saves as JSON string
Supertest test: PATCH with enriched_tags as 'a, b, c' saves as '["a","b","c"]'
Negative case: PATCH /api/assets/nonexistent returns 404 { error: 'Asset not found' }
Negative case: PATCH with empty body returns 400 { error: 'No fields provided' }

stories.title: AssetDetailModal — enriched-first view with Show Original toggle
stories.description: As a content author, I want to see enriched metadata by default in the asset detail so the best available data is front and centre.
acceptanceCriteria: AssetDetailModal default view: single-column metadata table showing enriched value if present, else original value, else grey italic 'Not enriched'
Add a 'Show Original' toggle button in the top-right of the metadata panel (navy outline button, small)
When 'Show Original' is active: render two-column layout with headers 'Original' and 'Enriched' (existing side-by-side layout)
Toggle button label changes to 'Hide Original' when active
Metadata rows shown: Title, Description, Tags, Location, Creator, Rights Owner, Usage Terms, Channel, Format
Enriched values displayed in navy text (#001B6B); empty enriched fields show '—' in grey
Toggle state resets to hidden (enriched-only) each time the modal is opened
Vitest test: modal renders enriched_title as primary title when present
Vitest test: 'Show Original' button is present in the metadata panel
Vitest test: clicking 'Show Original' button renders both 'Original' and 'Enriched' column headers
Negative case: asset with no enriched data at all shows original values with a 'Not yet enriched' banner and 'Enrich with AI' button (existing behaviour preserved)

stories.title: UploadModal — Review step with editable enrichment cards
stories.description: As a content author, I want to review and edit AI-proposed metadata for my uploaded images before saving so I can ensure accuracy before assets go into the library.
acceptanceCriteria: UploadModal adds a third step: Review (after Upload and Enriching progress)
Auto-advances to Review step when all files have reached a terminal status (done, error, or enrichment-unavailable)
Review step renders a scrollable list of ReviewCard components — one per successfully uploaded file

ReviewCard shows: thumbnail (150px square, using /api/assets/media/<thumbnail_path> or a placeholder), filename as card header, and editable fields: Title (text input), Description (textarea), Tags (text input, comma-separated), Location (text input)
Each field is pre-filled with AI-enriched values from the uploaded asset record
Each ReviewCard has a 'Save' button — on click calls updateAsset(id, editedFields), shows spinner during save, then green checkmark; card becomes read-only after save
Review step header shows: 'Review & Edit Enriched Metadata' and 'X images ready for review'
'Submit All' button at top of review list — on click saves all unsaved cards in sequence; button shows 'Saving X of Y...' during progress
After all saved: shows 'Done! X assets added to library.' with 'View Assets' button that closes modal and triggers asset grid refresh
Failed-upload cards shown greyed out with 'Upload failed' label — no editable fields, no Save button
Enrichment-unavailable cards shown with original filename and a note 'Saved without AI metadata' — fields editable, can still be submitted
Vitest test: ReviewCard renders pre-filled title input with enriched_title value
Vitest test: editing title input and clicking Save calls updateAsset with new title
Vitest test: Submit All button calls updateAsset for each unsaved card
Vitest test: successfully saved card shows checkmark and inputs become disabled
Negative case: if Save fails for a card, shows error message on that card and re-enables the Save button — does not block other cards
